title: Auditor prompt forbids claiming a full audit when coverage is incomplete
expectedSnippets: Do not claim full_audit unless every major discovered surface is directly reviewed
Maintain discoveredSurfaces as the canonical coverage ledger
forbiddenSnippets: claim full_audit after spot-checking only a subset of surfaces

title: Auditor prompt separates confirmed defects from likely risks and process gaps
expectedSnippets: Use confidence confirmed only when the cited evidence directly supports the conclusion.
Distinguish product defects from hardening advice and process/reporting mismatches.
forbiddenSnippets: flatten all findings into a single undifferentiated list

title: Audit command downgrades unsupported full-review claims and stays outside the execution runtime
expectedSnippets: Treat this command as a dedicated audit surface, not as Flow planning or feature execution.
If coverage is incomplete, downgrade achievedDepth honestly and explain the gap.
persist the final audit through flow_audit_write_report
forbiddenSnippets: start Flow execution to complete an audit

title: Audit contract requires explicit reviewed and unreviewed surface accounting
expectedSnippets: discoveredSurfaces: { name: string, category: source_runtime | tests | ci_release | docs_config | tooling | other, reviewStatus: directly_reviewed | spot_checked | unreviewed, evidence?: string[], reason?: string }[]
coverageRubric: { fullAuditEligible: boolean, directlyReviewedCategories: string[], spotCheckedCategories: string[], unreviewedCategories: string[], blockingReasons: string[] }
discoveredSurfaces is the canonical coverage ledger
forbiddenSnippets: omit unreviewed surfaces from the audit report
